_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 8 verified claims. 3R-race+religion: highest verification bar. Generic 'tabled Shariah-jurisdiction expansion bills' replaced with specific events: Kelantan moved to re-enact Nik Elin (2024) voided provisions; Kedah passed Syariah Court institutional amendments. Polling discussion sharpened: Pew (2013) 86% sharia-favourable + Merdeka narrower hudud findings; abandoned the over-simplified 'wide gap between Islamic values and full hudud'. 'Most Malay-Muslims want neither extreme' (UNVERIFIED — no poll defining the two extremes and showing majority rejection) replaced with empirically grounded 'broader sharia-in-principle support but narrower hudud support'. 'Middle ground has no political champion' commentary recast as 'whether any party builds a programme around it'.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Context 'Several state assemblies have tabled Shariah-jurisdiction expansion bills' replaced with specific verified events: Kelantan Nik Elin re-enactment motion + Kedah 2024 Syariah Court institutional amendments.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook sub specified the actual state actions rather than generic 'expansion bills'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 1 sub Nik Elin (2024) anchored as the verified constitutional case voiding Kelantan provisions.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'The gap between Islamic values and full hudud is wide in survey data' (oversimplified) → 'Pew (2013) found 86% favoured sharia as official law; Merdeka polls show narrower hudud support'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 3 sub 'Polling consistently shows broad sympathy ... much narrower support' replaced with anchored 'Support for sharia in principle is broad; support for hudud criminal-law implementation is narrower and varies with question framing'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — Reframe 'Most Malay-Muslims want neither extreme. The middle ground has no political champion' → 'broader sharia-in-principle support but narrower hudud support — a middle ground that resists either binary label' (poll-grounded, not opinion-asserted).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'The constitutional middle ground is where most voters sit, and where no party currently camps' → 'the political question is whether any party builds a programme around it' (frames as open question, not asserted fact).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): PAS governs Kelantan, Terengganu, Kedah; anchors PN's Perlis; Federal Constitution limits Shariah courts to State List; Federal Court rulings affirm civil-law supremacy where state enactments overreach; Malay-Muslim attitudes not monolithic; constitutional dual-jurisdiction debate is the real frame.
